content.method: Natural-experiment analysis of jurisdictions/periods where track capacity was constrained (e.g., Tiszavasvári-type denials, Encs expansion) tracking subsequent enrollment flows into church/private schools and tutoring-market indicators; supplement with parent survey/administrative data on displaced applicants' destinations.
content.question: If early selective tracks were delayed or abolished, how much of current segregation would be displaced into primary-school choice discretion, church/private admissions, private tutoring markets, or residential sorting, versus genuinely reduced?
content.why_it_matters: This determines whether structural reform (T3) or gatekeeping extensions (T6) produce net equity gains or merely relocate sorting to less-regulated channels—directly affecting whether T2's state-sector-only jurisdiction is a fatal design flaw and whether T6's cross-maintainer reach is necessary and sufficient.
